item.title: Intro to data visualization - R-Ladies Philly
item.description:
Chapter: R-Ladies Philly

When: Wednesday, May 6, 2026 at 10:00 PM UTC

Where: Parkway Branch of the Free Library of Philadelphia

Attendees: 2 going

BYOLaptop and learn the grammar of graphics in R using ggplot2. Join us in person or on Zoom for a hybrid workshop, where you’ll explore the power of data visualization in R.

View event details
item.author: noreply@rladies.org (R-Ladies Philly)

item.title: Intro to tidyverse - R-Ladies Philly
item.description:
Chapter: R-Ladies Philly

When: Wednesday, April 29, 2026 at 10:00 PM UTC

Where: Parkway Branch of the Free Library of Philadelphia

Attendees: 1 going

BYOLaptop to learn data wrangling skills! Join us for a hybrid meeting either in-person or on zoom where participants will login to a virtual R session and learn to use Tidyverse packages and functions.

View event details
item.author: noreply@rladies.org (R-Ladies Philly)

item.title: Writing reports and articles with R in Quarto - R-Ladies Trondheim
item.description:
Chapter: R-Ladies Trondheim

When: Tuesday, April 28, 2026 at 3:00 PM UTC

Where: Room ØHM, Øya helsehus

Attendees: 6 going

Quarto provides a convenient way to automatically create reports with results and figures generated from R-code. It can play an important role in a reproducible research workflow, and saves time since whenever the results change, the report is automatically up to date. It is a crucial tool in any R-lady's toolbox! Join us for an introductory tutorial in how to get the most use out of Quarto. You can find a map for the room here: https://link.mazemap.com/UKTLR7Tu

View event details
item.author: noreply@rladies.org (R-Ladies Trondheim)

item.title: Career panel and networking - R-Ladies Philly
item.description:
Chapter: R-Ladies Philly

When: Wednesday, April 15, 2026 at 10:00 PM UTC

Where: Parkway Branch of the Free Library of Philadelphia

Attendees: 3 going

Join us for a career panel and networking with established professionals who use R statistical programming language for data analysis in their career.

View event details
item.author: noreply@rladies.org (R-Ladies Philly)

item.title: Save the Date: Maps in R - R-Ladies St. Louis
item.description:
Chapter: R-Ladies St. Louis

When: Thursday, April 9, 2026 at 11:00 PM UTC

Where: Online event

Attendees: 19 going

This will be a virtual event. Description is TBD. Topic is making maps in R.

View event details
item.author: noreply@rladies.org (R-Ladies St. Louis)

item.title: ChatGPT in RStudio with gptstudio: Better than GitHub Copilot? - R-Ladies St. Louis
item.description:
Chapter: R-Ladies St. Louis

When: Friday, March 27, 2026 at 3:00 PM UTC

Where: Online event

Attendees: 51 going

**Supercharging Your R Workflow with a Native AI Assistant** It’s no surprise that large language models like ChatGPT are helpful for coding. What makes gptstudio stand out is how it brings that help directly into RStudio through a clean, user-friendly add-in. gptstudio is an R package that integrates ChatGPT into RStudio, allowing you to generate code, explain functions, and write documentation using natural language, all without leaving your IDE. It supports various OpenAI models and offers an add-in panel that works like a built-in assistant. This tool is especially useful when you're experimenting, writing boilerplate code, or just need quick answers while staying in your flow. Advantages of using the gptstudio package: ✔️ Seamless integration into RStudio via an add-in panel ✔️ Helps generate and explain R code using plain language prompts ✔️ Supports multiple OpenAI models, including GPT-3.5 and GPT-4 ✔️ Useful for both beginners and experienced users looking to speed up their workflow

View event details
item.author: noreply@rladies.org (R-Ladies St. Louis)

item.title: R-Ladies Corvallis: R Show & Tell - R-Ladies Corvallis
item.description:
Chapter: R-Ladies Corvallis

When: Friday, March 13, 2026 at 12:30 AM UTC

Where: Common Fields

Attendees: 4 going

**Learn how other R-Ladies in the community are using R!** At our second meet-up, we'll be hosting an informal Show & Tell. You can present in the Show & Tell or just come and hang out. For the Show & Tell portion, we're asking anyone who is interested in sharing to create a single slide highlighting anything related to how they use R. For example, share how you use R in your work, tell us about a cool R project you've worked on, or share something you want to learn more about. The current plan is to ask presenters to talk for 5 minutes. *We may adjust the time limit depending on how many volunteers we get.* **If you are interested in presenting during the Show & Tell, please [fill out this brief form](https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSeQwuwjWZK3vIc6VHP3PIlw4xhXa4Abag3Cs7gh5SYDoz3c5g/viewform?usp=publish-editor).** We will send details and instructions for the informal presentations on March 4. If you would like to present, please fill out the form by **March 3**. **You do not need to present during the Show & Tell to attend this event**. If you'd rather just listen, hang out, and chat, you are encouraged to still attend! Food and drink are available for purchase. Please note this event will be held outside. We have a pavilion reserved, but we recommend bundling up just in case it's cold! This is an open and informal event! Come as you are - any and all R skill level and experience welcome.

View event details
item.author: noreply@rladies.org (R-Ladies Corvallis)

item.title: Create Custom Webpages with R and Quarto - R-Ladies Vancouver
item.description:
Chapter: R-Ladies Vancouver

When: Wednesday, March 4, 2026 at 3:00 AM UTC

Where: Online event

Attendees: 20 going

**Workshop Details** In this workshop, we will learn how to create webpages using Quarto in RStudio, and host it on GitHub. The example website will be a professional website template ([see my example webpage here](https://grcetmpk.github.io/)), but these skills can also be used to create other webpages ([like this one](https://ubc-stat.github.io/stat545/)). This workshop was designed for R coders with no prior experience with Quarto. Experience with GitHub is helpful, but not necessary. We will be going through all of the steps slowly. \*\*\* 1. If this is your first R-Ladies event, please take a moment to review our R-Ladies Global code of conduct: [https://github.com/rladies/starter-kit/wiki/Code-of-Conduct](https://github.com/rladies/starter-kit/wiki/Code-of-Conduct) 2. Who is this allowed to attend the event? Everyone is welcome to join us at our events and to be a part of our mission to empower gender minorities with the skills and knowledge to code in one of the top programming language in data science, which is R. 3\. To fulfill this mission we do ask participants to respect a focus on the encouragement and support for women and non\-binary people during our events\. See you there! Jasmine, and Melissa

View event details
item.author: noreply@rladies.org (R-Ladies Vancouver)

item.title: Coffee and Conversation - R-Ladies St. Louis
item.description:
Chapter: R-Ladies St. Louis

When: Saturday, February 28, 2026 at 4:30 PM UTC

Where: Webster Groves Public Library

Attendees: 19 going

Join us for a morning of conversation and networking with fellow women in data. If you’re interested in data careers, looking to hire from underrepresented groups in data, or wanting to brainstorm about a project you’ve been working on, we’d love to see you. This is an in-person event and refreshments will be provided, so we appreciate your RSVP.

View event details
item.author: noreply@rladies.org (R-Ladies St. Louis)

item.author: noreply@rladies.org (R-Ladies Abuja)

item.title: From Incident to Solution: How IT Functional Support Keeps the Business Running - R Ladies Tirane
item.description:
Chapter: R Ladies Tirane

When: Monday, February 23, 2026 at 5:00 PM UTC

Where: Online event

Attendees: 14 going

Open Lecture: **"From Incident to Solution: How IT Functional Support Keeps the Business Running"** Part of SPARK series IT incidents don’t just affect systems, they impact operations, safety, and business continuity. In this session, we’ll explore how IT Functional Application Support turns incidents into solutions and plays a key role in keeping maritime operations running smoothly. **🎤 Speaker: Rinalda Prifti** **Functional Applications Support, Smit Lamnalco Netherlands** 📅 Date: February 23, 2026 ⏰ Time: 18:00 📍 Format: Online (Zoom) 👉 Register on Meetup to receive the Zoom link and all the details.

View event details
item.author: noreply@rladies.org (R Ladies Tirane)
